Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

The Virtual Temple - chanting

Social · asked by user [] · 2007-12-20 · 7 answers
Hare Krishna,

can anyone tell me where to actually find the link to do the group japa? Am I missing it in this article?

http://namahatta.org/en/node/4065

Thanks in advance,
yhs
Hari Bhakta dasa
user [75] · 2007-12-20
im sorry, at some point the link must have disappeared from the article; im pretty sure it was there originally. kripamoya prabhu refers to the web site "vaisnava connection," at http://vconline.biz/
user [33] · 2007-12-20
Thank you prabhu. I found a link but it leads to a site that is for the London devotees only but perhaps it has not been updated to include everyone. I wrote and asked. Ill let you know.
user [2] · 2007-12-20
I tried to enroll a few months ago, but seems to be for local devotees, they ask a lot of questions about who is your sponsor etc in the enroll form as I recall.
I just copy and paste here:

Sign up here to join the
ISKCON UK
Online Community
Morning Programme

Please note: this service is only for Kripamoya prabhus namahatta members and ISKCON congregation members in the UK .
user [33] · 2007-12-21
I recieved an answer from them and yes, they asked a lot of questions. I wrote back and told them that I do not live near a temple and had understand from a Chakra as that this program was open to everyone desiring to chant and associate. Ill keep you updated with their answer.
user [166] · 2007-12-21
Antardwipa prabhu who runs the vconline site let me join (I reside in Australia) so it is possible to join from other countries...alternatively there is the Japa Room starting in mid January 2008: http://www.japaroom.com
user [33] · 2007-12-24
Got a response today from Virtual Temple. It is only open for European devotees due to the time difference.

I cant get the japaroom page to open. I was looking more for the Virtual Temple because it is more like being with the other devotees chanting. What type of program would you use to set something like that up?
user [166] · 2007-12-27
We are using PaltalkScene....the idea of the Japa Room is to improve the quality of your own Japa by way of an easy 10 stage program...please try the site now it is working.
